engineIntelligence.h2: MG Metro Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the MG Metro in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: MG Metro A-Plus 12H engine - petrol 1.3L replacement UK
productionHistory: MG Metro City X, HLE, Mayfair - 1984-1990
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - thermal cycling causes MLS gasket degradation, typically after 70k miles.
Timing chain rattle - tensioner wear leads to chain slap and potential jump on high-mileage units.
Overheating - classic cooling system layout struggles in modern traffic without upgrades.

cards.altText: MG Metro A-Series 998 engine - petrol 1.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil consumption - piston ring wear increases blue smoke after 80k miles on higher-mileage units.
Carburettor jetting issues - HIF38 carb requires periodic adjustment for smooth running.

cards.altText: MG Metro Turbo A-Plus engine - petrol 1.3L turbo replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbocharger wear - Garrett T3 bearings degrade, causing boost loss and oil consumption.
Wastegate calibration drift - mechanical wastegate requires specialist adjustment to restore 93PS output.
Head gasket under boost - increased cylinder pressure accelerates gasket failure on neglected units.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £2,450-£3,200 (supply only) Estimated from comparable A-Plus Turbo units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: MG Metro Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the MG Metro across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: Base engine for early MG Metro L models; simple carburetted design favoured by classic restorers for ease of rebuild.
commonFailures: Oil consumption from piston ring wear - typically evident after 70k-80k miles on higher-mileage units.
Carburettor jetting drift - HIF38 carb requires periodic adjustment for smooth idle and throttle response.
entries.cta: Get quotes for A-Series 998 MG Metro engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Upgraded A-Series with stronger internals; powered most MG Metro 1300 variants and became the backbone of the range.
compatibleVariants: MG Metro 1300, MG Metro City X, MG Metro HLE, MG Metro Mayfair, MG Metro S, MG Metro Vanden Plas
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - thermal cycling degrades standard gasket; MLS upgrade recommended during rebuild.
Timing chain tensioner wear - causes rattle and potential jump; preventative replacement advised at 30k miles.
Cooling system overheating - classic layout struggles in modern traffic without radiator/hose upgrades.
entries.cta: Get quotes for A-Plus 12H MG Metro engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol Turbocharged
entries.history: Iconic hot-hatch powerplant; Garrett T3 turbo and mechanical wastegate defined the MG Metro Turbo's 93PS performance.
compatibleVariants: MG Metro Turbo, MG Metro Turbo Intercooler
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear - Garrett T3 units degrade with age, causing boost loss and oil consumption.
Wastegate calibration drift - mechanical wastegate requires specialist adjustment to restore original boost characteristics.
Head gasket under boost - increased cylinder pressure accelerates failure on neglected or poorly maintained units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for A-Plus Turbo MG Metro engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: MG Metro Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The MG Metro's A-Series and A-Plus petrol engines are generally robust but prone to classic failure patterns like head gasket degradation and timing chain wear. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded MLS gaskets and cooling system inspection to address original weak points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: MG Metro Head Gasket Failure - Why A-Series Engines Leak Coolant
problems.affectedModels: MG Metro 1300, City X, HLE, Mayfair, S, Vanden Plas, Turbo (1982-1990, A-Plus 12H & Turbo variants)
problems.rootCause: Thermal cycling causes standard composite gasket to degrade; classic cooling system layout exacerbates hot spots under modern traffic conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: MLS gasket replacement + cooling system flush + thermostat check
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before overheating damage; adds 20-30k miles reliability

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with upgraded MLS gasket, cooling inspection, bench testing, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Metros; addresses root cause and restores reliability

problems.recommendation: On a classic MG Metro worth £3,000-£8,000, a preventative gasket upgrade makes sense if the engine is otherwise sound. If overheating has already occurred or mileage exceeds 80k, a rebuilt A-Plus engine with warranty offers better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt A-Plus 12H engine prices for your MG Metro

problems.h4: MG Metro Overheating - Cooling System Failure & Prevention
problems.affectedModels: All MG Metro variants with A-Series/A-Plus engines (1982-1990)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 70,000+ miles or after prolonged modern-traffic use
problems.rootCause: Front-mounted radiator with belt-driven water pump struggles with stop-start driving; original hoses and thermostat housing degrade with age.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Radiator flush, new hoses, thermostat replacement, water pump inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for Metros with otherwise healthy engines; prevents recurrence if maintained

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine + Cooling Bundle
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components (radiator, hoses, thermostat housing) + warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Ideal for daily-driver restorations; addresses root cause and adds modern reliability

problems.recommendation: If your MG Metro overheats but the engine hasn't seized, a cooling system refresh is cost-effective. However, if overheating has caused head warping or gasket failure, investing in a rebuilt engine with cooling upgrades delivers better value for a car you plan to keep.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MG Metro cooling system upgrade with rebuilt engine

problems.h4: MG Metro Timing Chain Rattle - A-Series Wear & Prevention
problems.affectedModels: MG Metro 1300, Turbo, City X, HLE, Mayfair, S, Vanden Plas (1982-1990, A-Plus 12H variants)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain slap; neglected service intervals accelerate wear and risk chain jump causing valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing chain, tensioner, guide replacement + valve timing reset
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; recommended every 30k miles on classic A-Series

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt engine with new timing components, bench testing, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if chain has jumped or caused valve/piston contact damage

problems.recommendation: Timing chain service is one of the most cost-effective preventative measures for MG Metro owners. If you hear rattle but the engine still runs smoothly, book a timing service immediately. If the chain has jumped, a rebuilt engine is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare MG Metro A-Plus timing service vs rebuilt engine prices

problems.h4: MG Metro Turbo Boost Loss - Garrett T3 Wear & Calibration
problems.affectedModels: MG Metro Turbo, Turbo Intercooler (1983-1990, A-Plus Turbo variants)
problems.rootCause: Garrett T3 turbocharger bearings wear with age; mechanical wastegate calibration drifts, reducing boost pressure and performance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbocharger overhaul, wastegate calibration, boost testing, oil feed inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for Turbos with otherwise healthy bottom end; restores near-original performance

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Turbo A-Plus Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt turbo engine with new Garrett T3, wastegate calibration, dyno testing, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for collector-grade Turbos or units with additional bottom-end wear

problems.recommendation: For MG Metro Turbo owners focused on preserving original performance, a specialist turbo rebuild with wastegate calibration offers excellent value. If the engine has high mileage or additional wear, a complete rebuilt turbo A-Plus engine with warranty provides collector-grade reliability.
problems.cta: Request MG Metro Turbo specialist rebuild quote with wastegate calibration

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your MG Metro
finalCta.paragraph: The MG Metro's classic A-Series engines reward proactive maintenance, but when failure strikes, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing original weak points, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. With nationwide delivery and supply & fit options, you can restore your iconic hot hatch without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare MG Metro Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific MG Metro and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: MG Metro Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the MG Metro is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: MG Metro 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0L A-Series petrol engine powered the entry-level MG Metro L during early production (1982-1985). As the smallest displacement option, it offered modest power but excellent simplicity for classic restoration projects and budget-conscious rebuilds.
commonFailurePoints: Oil consumption from piston ring wear; HIF38 carburettor jetting drift requiring periodic adjustment
items.cta: Compare MG Metro 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: MG Metro 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L A-Plus petrol engine formed the backbone of the MG Metro range from 1982-1990, powering the popular 1300 and most trim variants. Its robust design and widespread parts availability make it the most commonly replaced engine for Metro restoration projects.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket degradation from thermal cycling; timing chain tensioner wear; classic cooling system overheating in modern traffic
items.cta: Get quotes for MG Metro 1.3L petrol engines

items.title: MG Metro 1.3L Turbo Petrol Engines
items.description: The turbocharged 1.3L A-Plus engine defined the iconic MG Metro Turbo (1983-1990), delivering 93PS via a Garrett T3 turbocharger and mechanical wastegate. Specialist rebuilds preserving original boost characteristics are highly sought by collectors and performance enthusiasts.
commonFailurePoints: Garrett T3 turbo bearing wear; wastegate calibration drift; head gasket failure under boost pressure on neglected units
items.cta: Enquire about MG Metro 1.3L turbo petrol engine availability

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your MG Metro needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK MG Metro specialists.

engineYears.h2: MG Metro Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the MG Metro's 1982-1990 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: Launch years introduced the base A-Series 998cc and upgraded A-Plus 1275cc engines that would define the Metro range.
keyChanges: MG Metro launched with A-Series 998cc base engine and A-Plus 1275cc for 1300 variants
HIF38 carburettor fitted to early production models
MG Metro Turbo introduced in 1983 with Garrett T3 turbocharged A-Plus
mainEngines: A-Plus Turbo - 1.3L turbo petrol (MG Metro Turbo)
knownFor: Simple carburetted design favoured by classic restorers and DIY rebuilders
Early head gasket failures on A-Plus units led to MLS upgrade recommendations
Turbo variant established MG Metro as an iconic 1980s hot hatch
years.cta: Get quotes for 1982-1984 MG Metro replacement engines
years.ticker: MG Metro head gasket replacement • MG Metro 1300 engine rebuild • MG Metro Turbo specialist rebuild

years.description: Facelift models standardised the A-Plus 1275cc across most variants with HIF44 carburettor and refined ancillaries.

A-Plus 1275cc became standard across 1300, City X, HLE, Mayfair, S and Vanden Plas variants
Turbo variant continued with specialist calibration requirements for collector market

A-Plus Turbo - 1.3L turbo petrol (MG Metro Turbo, Turbo Intercooler)
knownFor: HIF44 carburettor requires specific jetting verification during engine swap
Cooling system upgrades increasingly recommended for modern traffic reliability
Late-production Turbos highly sought by collectors; specialist rebuilds preserve original 93PS output

faq.h2: MG Metro Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does MG Metro engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: MG Metro engine replacement typically costs £995-£3,200 for a rebuilt unit supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. The A-Series 998cc starts around £995, A-Plus 1275cc from £1,245, and Turbo specialist rebuilds from £2,450.
keyPoints: A-Series 998cc rebuilt: £995-£1,650 supply only
A-Plus 1275cc rebuilt: £1,245-£2,100 supply only
Turbo A-Plus specialist rebuild: £2,450-£3,200 supply only
Supply & fit adds £350-£650 depending on location
All prices include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare MG Metro engine replacement prices now

items.question: What engine codes fit MG Metro models?
items.answer: MG Metro models use A-Series 998cc (early L variants) or A-Plus 12H 1275cc (most 1300 and trim variants). Turbo models use the A-Plus Turbo code with Garrett T3 turbocharger and mechanical wastegate.
keyPoints: A-Series 998: early MG Metro L (1982-1985)
A-Plus 12H 1275cc: MG Metro 1300, City X, HLE, Mayfair, S, Vanden Plas
A-Plus Turbo: MG Metro Turbo and Turbo Intercooler (1983-1990)
Verify carburettor type (HIF38/HIF44) before ordering replacement
items.cta: Check your MG Metro engine code compatibility instantly

items.question: What causes MG Metro head gasket failure?
items.answer: Thermal cycling degrades the standard composite head gasket on A-Series/A-Plus engines. Classic cooling system layout exacerbates hot spots, especially in modern stop-start traffic without upgrades.
keyPoints: Standard gasket material degrades with repeated heat cycles
Cooling system inefficiency creates localised hot spots
MLS (multi-layer steel) gasket upgrade recommended during rebuild
Overheating damage may require full engine replacement
items.cta: Get quotes for MG Metro head gasket replacement with MLS upgrade

items.question: Is MG Metro Turbo engine replacement worth it?
items.answer: Yes for collectors and enthusiasts - the MG Metro Turbo's iconic status and limited production make specialist rebuilds a sound investment for preserving performance and value.
keyPoints: Turbo variants command premium resale values (£5k-£15k for good examples)
Specialist rebuilds preserve original 93PS output and boost characteristics
Wastegate calibration and turbo inspection essential for authentic performance
12-month warranty protects your investment in a collector-grade engine
items.cta: Request MG Metro Turbo specialist rebuild quote today

items.question: How long does MG Metro engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engines typically dispatch within 3-5 working days from stock. Supply & fit services with accredited specialists usually complete within 5-10 working days including delivery and commissioning.
keyPoints: Stock rebuilt engines: 3-5 day dispatch from UK suppliers
Non-stock or Turbo specialist rebuilds: 10-21 day lead time
Supply & fit: 5-10 days including delivery, fitting and road test
Next-day delivery available on selected stock A-Plus units
items.cta: Check live MG Metro engine stock and delivery times

items.question: Can I upgrade my MG Metro engine during replacement?
items.answer: Yes - common upgrades include 998cc to 1275cc A-Plus swap, MLS head gasket, cooling system enhancements, and timing component upgrades for improved reliability in modern use.
keyPoints: 998cc to 1275cc A-Plus swap is straightforward with compatible mounts
MLS head gasket and upgraded cooling components recommended for all rebuilds
Timing chain tensioner upgrade adds preventative reliability
Turbo intercooler piping available for performance-focused builds
items.cta: Discuss MG Metro engine upgrade options with our specialists

items.question: What warranty comes with MG Metro replacement engines?
items.answer: All rebuilt, reconditioned and remanufactured MG Metro engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour at accredited UK fitting centres.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty valid at accredited classic car fitting centres UK-wide
Used engines may have shorter or supplier-specific warranty terms
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Compare MG Metro engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: How do I identify my MG Metro engine code?
items.answer: Find the engine code stamped on the cylinder block near the distributor or on the cylinder head. Cross-reference with your registration via DVLA lookup for instant compatibility verification before ordering.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near distributor or on cylinder head
Carburettor model (HIF38/HIF44) also critical for fuel system matching
DVLA registration lookup provides instant variant and engine verification
Photo of code stamp helpful when requesting quotes from specialists
items.cta: Use our free MG Metro engine code checker tool now

items.question: Are used MG Metro engines reliable?
items.answer: Used MG Metro engines can be reliable if sourced from low-mileage, well-maintained donors with verified service history. Always request a compression test report and inspect for common failure signs before purchase.
keyPoints: Request compression test report to assess internal engine health
Verify service history and mileage via DVLA/supplier documentation
Budget for immediate gasket/seal refresh after installation
Rebuilt engines with warranty often better long-term value than undocumented used units
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt MG Metro engine options and pricing

items.question: Where can I find MG Metro specialist engine fitters UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket's network includes accredited classic car specialists across the UK experienced in MG Metro A-Series swaps. Enter your postcode to find the nearest supplier offering supply-only or full supply & fit services.
keyPoints: Nationwide network of MG Metro-experienced classic car garages
Specialists trained on A-Series compatibility and Metro-specific fitment
Supply-only or full supply & fit options available depending on your needs
Single-point warranty support regardless of installation location
items.cta: Find your nearest MG Metro engine specialist now
